name: OpenAI Multi-Turn Chaining via previousResponseId
query: Build me a 3-turn OpenAI conversation workflow where each turn is a separate LLM_CHAT_COMPLETE task. I don't want to resend the message history on every turn — make it efficient. Walk me through what's happening and any provider lock-in I should be aware of.
expected_behavior: Step 1: Consult references/workflow-definition.md (LLM_CHAT_COMPLETE), examples/llm-chaining.md, and examples/llm-chat.md
Step 2: Recognize that this is the OpenAI Responses API pattern — use `previousResponseId` on turns 2 and 3 to chain without resending history
Step 3: Turn 1 includes the full system prompt + first user message in `messages` and does NOT have `previousResponseId`
Step 4: Turn 2's `messages` contains only the new user message; `previousResponseId: ${turn1.output.responseId}` references turn 1
Step 5: Turn 3's `messages` contains only the new user message; `previousResponseId: ${turn2.output.responseId}` references turn 2 (NOT turn 1 — each turn references the immediately preceding one)
Step 6: Use `{role, message}` schema (NOT `{role, content}`) on every message
Step 7: Explicitly warn about provider lock-in — previousResponseId is OpenAI/Azure-only, other providers silently ignore it; mid-chain provider switches break the chain
Step 8: Mention the responseId retention bound (~30 days per OpenAI's documented policy) and recommend accumulated-messages pattern for long-lived workflows
Step 9: Write the workflow JSON to a file before registration
success_criteria: Turn 1 contains the full initial system + user messages and does NOT set `previousResponseId`
Turns 2 and 3 each contain ONLY the new user message in `messages` — NOT the accumulated history
Turn 3 references `${turn2.output.responseId}` (the immediately preceding turn) as `previousResponseId` — NOT turn1's responseId
Every `messages` entry uses Conductor's `{role, message}` schema — NOT `{role, content}`
Agent explicitly states that `previousResponseId` is OpenAI/Azure-only (Responses API) and other providers ignore it
Agent mentions at least one of: the ~30-day responseId retention window, the no-provider-switch constraint, or the trade-off vs accumulated messages for portability
Both turns use the SAME `llmProvider` (`openai` or `azureopenai`) — agent does not mix providers mid-chain
Workflow JSON is written to a file before registration
